title: Perfector Node
description: Canonical entity with multiple projections: vectorial (recall), graph (relations/PageRank), documental (wiki/audit), and compressed (low-token context). All projections share a single perfector_id.

perfector_id.description: Stable canonical ID: {node_type}.{slug}. Unique across the entire knowledge base. Example: decision.use-duckdb-lock-retry

status.description: Lifecycle state. draft → canonical (after reuse threshold). canonical → superseded (when replaced).

tldr_32.description: Ultra-compressed summary (~32 words, ~40 tokens). Used for list views and quick context injection.

brief_96.description: Short summary (~96 words, ~120 tokens). Used for standard context and recall results.

summary_256.description: Extended summary (~256 words, ~320 tokens). Used when more context is needed without reading full content.

memory_id.description: Reference to the semantic memory entry (memories table UUID).

wiki_page.description: Relative path to the compiled wiki page. Example: .wiki/pages/decision-use-duckdb-lock-retry.md

raw_sources.description: Paths to archived raw source materials in .wiki/raw/.

target.description: perfector_id or node_id of the related entity.

tools.description: MCP tools relevant to this entity.

commands.description: Shell commands associated with this entity.

files.description: Source files involved in this entity.

execution.description: Execution context — which tools, commands, and files relate to this entity.

pagerank.description: PageRank score from the knowledge graph.

reuse_count.description: Number of times this entity was recalled/referenced.

confidence.description: Confidence score (0-1). Increases with validation and reuse.

created_at.description: When the entity was first created.

last_validated_at.description: When the entity was last confirmed as still accurate.

ttl_class.description: Time-to-live classification. ephemeral (minutes), session (hours), durable (days), canonical (permanent).

platform.description: Which platforms this entity applies to.
